objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-definition of reduced motion overrides. Cannot fully review motion system, component architecture, core layout shell, screen implementations, or API client — all listed as required deliverables.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the spec including: full animations.css, component architecture map, core layout shell component, 3 key screens (Dashboard/Home, Detail/Timeline, Action sheet/Form), API client implementation, and all hook implementations.

objections.description: Bundle size script uses Unix-specific `gzip -c | wc -c` pipeline via execSync. Will fail silently or crash on Windows CI runners. Also uses `import.meta.dirname` requiring Node.js 20.11+ without documenting the requirement.
objections.suggested_fix: Use Node's built-in `zlib.gzipSync()` for cross-platform gzip measurement, and `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` for dirname. Add `engines` field to package.json: `"engines": { "node": ">=20.11.0" }`.

objections.description: `validate_frontend_build()` is defined but never called. FastAPI won't automatically invoke it at startup. The startup guard won't actually guard anything.
objections.suggested_fix:
Add lifespan context or on_event hook:
```python
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    validate_frontend_build()
    yield
app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
```

objections.description: Script doesn't validate stored theme value. If localStorage contains corrupted data (e.g., 'null', undefined, or arbitrary string), it sets invalid theme and breaks styling. Also uses `var` declaration style inconsistent with rest of codebase.

Reduced motion media query only overrides `--duration-fast: 0ms` but leaves `--duration-normal`, `--duration-slow`, and `--duration-glacial` unchanged. Users with vestibular disorders will still experience motion.
Top-level await in plugin array requires ESM module context and may fail with certain build tooling. The conditional import pattern is fragile.
Double-ring uses `--color-bg-primary` as inner ring, creating inconsistent appearance on elevated surfaces (`--color-bg-elevated`) where the inner ring won't match the actual background.
Exception handler doesn't pass through existing response headers. If a 404 response had custom headers, they'll be lost.
objections.suggested_fix: Preserve headers from original exception where applicable, though this is minor since 404s rarely have custom headers.

suggestions.description: Add error handling for missing dist/assets directory to provide actionable error message instead of cryptic ENOENT.
Add `browserslist` configuration to package.json for explicit transpilation targets. Without it, Vite defaults may not match your intended browser support matrix.
Add `<meta name="color-scheme" content="dark light">` to help browsers with form controls and scrollbars theming.
Document the error boundary strategy for React components. Production-grade apps need graceful degradation when components fail, especially for the API client and async operations.
